member.summary: Explicit threat assumptions for hostile environment operation. These flags drive defensive behaviors throughout the system.

member.summary: Operating system may be compromised or monitored.

member.summary: Kernel-level threats assumed (rootkits, hypervisor attacks).

member.summary: Clipboard contents may be monitored or exfiltrated.

member.summary: Keystrokes may be logged by malware or hardware.

member.summary: Memory may be scraped by malware or cold boot attacks.

member.summary: Supply chain may be compromised (malicious packages, updates).

member.summary: AI tools may inadvertently leak secrets.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Attestor.ThreatAssumptions.ScreenCaptureRisk
member.summary: Screen may be captured or shoulder-surfed.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.DomainStores.AttestorStore
member.summary:
Attestor domain store - identity and authentication authority.
This store contains: - TOTP/HOTP seeds and configuration - Passkey credential metadata (private keys never leave this store) - Certificate metadata and encrypted private keys - SSH key metadata and encrypted private keys - Identity policies and bindings - Approval audit trail
This store does NOT contain: - Passwords or login credentials (Obscura domain) - Recovery codes (Recovery domain)
Encrypted with: K_attestor (domain key) File: attestor.store.encrypted
CRITICAL: Attestor performs cryptographic operations. Keys in this store are USED, never EXPORTED.

member.summary: Obscura domain - credential storage only. Contains passwords, notes, cards. Does NOT contain TOTP, passkeys, or recovery codes.

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.DomainStores.CryptoDomain.Attestor
member.summary: Attestor domain - identity and authentication authority. Contains TOTP seeds, passkey private keys, certificates, SSH keys. Performs cryptographic operations (signing, OTP generation). Keys are USED here, never EXPORTED.

member.summary: Recovery domain - break-glass recovery operations. Contains sealed recovery key blobs, recovery codes, audit log. HARDER to unlock than other domains (not a convenience layer).

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.DomainStores.ObscuraStore
member.summary:
Obscura domain store - encrypted vault/credential storage ONLY.
This store contains: - Credential entries (passwords, notes, cards, etc.) - Categories for organization - Vault metadata (name, description)
This store does NOT contain: - TOTP secrets (moved to Attestor) - Passkey data (moved to Attestor) - Recovery codes (moved to Recovery)
Encrypted with: K_obscura (domain key) File: obscura.store.encrypted

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.DomainStores.RecoveryStore
member.summary:
Recovery domain store - break-glass recovery operations.
This store contains: - Sealed recovery key blobs (one per domain) - Recovery policy (factors, delays, version pins) - Recovery audit log - Recovery code hashes (single-use)
This store does NOT contain: - Plaintext master keys - Plaintext domain keys - Exportable passkey private keys - Anything auto-readable by Obscura or Attestor
Encrypted with: K_recovery (domain key) File: recovery.store.encrypted
CRITICAL: Recovery is HARDER to unlock than anything else. Recovery is NOT a convenience layer.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.EncryptionAlgorithm
member.summary: Defines constants for supported encryption algorithms. The manifest stores one of these values in its Algorithm property to indicate which cipher was used to encrypt its contents. Use these constants when setting or comparing algorithms to avoid spelling mistakes.

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.EncryptionAlgorithm.Aes256Gcm
member.summary: AES‑256 in Galois/Counter Mode with a 96‑bit nonce and 128‑bit authentication tag. Widely considered the gold standard for symmetric encryption as of 2025.

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.EncryptionAlgorithm.XChaCha20Poly1305
member.summary: XChaCha20 stream cipher combined with Poly1305 MAC. Provides a 192‑bit nonce for easier random nonce generation and is suitable for long messages. Not currently implemented but reserved for future use.

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.EncryptionAlgorithm.KyberAesHybrid
member.summary: Hybrid post‑quantum scheme combining CRYSTALS‑Kyber for key encapsulation with AES‑256‑GCM for payload encryption. Only a stub in this implementation; real Kyber support requires an external library.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.PasskeyCredential
member.summary: Represents a WebAuthn/FIDO2 passkey credential. Contains the credential ID and public key for authentication.

member.summary: Unique identifier for this credential.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.PasskeyCredential.PublicKey
member.summary: Public key for signature verification.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.PasskeyCredential.CreatedAt
member.summary: When the credential was created.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.PasskeyCredential.Name
member.summary: Display name for the credential.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.PasswordHealthReport
member.summary: Summarises the results of a password health analysis. Counters track how many credentials are considered weak (entropy below a threshold), old (not updated for a defined duration) or reused across multiple entries. A list of titles for problematic credentials can be provided for display.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy
member.summary: AI-aware access policy that actively defends against AI leakage. Unlike most vaults that ignore AI risks, Phantom explicitly blocks secret exposure to AI tools while remaining AI-aware for future features.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.Version
member.summary: Version of the AI access policy schema.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.AllowAiAccess
member.summary: Master switch: when false (default), all AI access is blocked. This is the secure default - AI must be explicitly enabled.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.BlockClipboardToAi
member.summary: Block clipboard sharing to detected AI tool contexts. Prevents accidental paste of secrets into AI chat interfaces.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.BlockAutofillToAi
member.summary: Block autofill into browser contexts flagged as AI chat interfaces. Detected via URL patterns, DOM analysis, or known AI service domains.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.EnableAiSafeView
member.summary: Enable AI-safe view mode: shows masked placeholders instead of raw values. Useful when screen sharing or working near AI transcription tools.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.AiSafePlaceholder
member.summary: When AI-safe view is active, show this placeholder instead of secrets.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.BlockedAiDomains
member.summary: Detected AI service domains to block. Updated periodically.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.CustomBlockedDomains
member.summary: Additional user-defined domains to block for AI access.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.AiWindowPatterns
member.summary: Window title patterns that indicate AI context (for clipboard blocking).

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.LogAiAccessAttempts
member.summary: Log all AI access attempts for audit purposes.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.RequireAiConsentPrompt
member.summary: Require explicit user confirmation before any AI-related operation.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.AllowAiMetadataAccess
member.summary: Future: allow AI to access non-sensitive metadata only.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AIAccessPolicy.AllowedAiServices
member.summary: Future: specific AI services that may be granted limited access.

member.summary: Timestamp when policy was last updated.

member.summary: Trusted corporate environment with managed devices.

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.ThreatEnvironment.Standard

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.ThreatEnvironment.HostileOS
member.summary: Operating system may be compromised or monitored.

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.ThreatEnvironment.UntrustedKernel
member.summary: Kernel-level threats assumed (rootkits, hypervisor attacks).

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.ThreatEnvironment.PhysicalCompromise
member.summary: Physical access threats (evil maid, device seizure).

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.ThreatEnvironment.AdversarialEnvironment
member.summary: Maximum paranoia: all layers potentially compromised.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AssumedThreats
member.summary: Explicit threat assumptions for the vault environment. Setting these to true enables corresponding defensive behaviors.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AssumedThreats.ClipboardCompromise
member.summary: Assume clipboard contents may be monitored or exfiltrated. Enables: clipboard clearing, no clipboard autofill, masked copy.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AssumedThreats.KeyloggerPresent
member.summary: Assume keystrokes may be logged by malware or hardware. Enables: on-screen keyboard option, keystroke obfuscation.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AssumedThreats.MemoryScrapeRisk
member.summary: Assume memory may be scraped by malware or cold boot attacks. Enables: aggressive memory zeroing, reduced key lifetime.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AssumedThreats.ScreenCaptureRisk
member.summary: Assume screen may be captured or shoulder-surfed. Enables: masked display by default, no preview thumbnails.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AssumedThreats.NetworkInterception
member.summary: Assume network traffic may be intercepted or modified. Enables: certificate pinning, no network features.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AssumedThreats.SupplyChainRisk
member.summary: Assume supply chain may be compromised (malicious packages, updates). Enables: build verification, dependency hash checking.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AssumedThreats.AiLeakageRisk
member.summary: Assume AI tools may inadvertently leak secrets via prompts. Enables: AI access blocking, clipboard isolation from AI contexts.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AssumedThreats.BrowserExtensionRisk
member.summary: Assume browser extensions may intercept autofill data. Enables: extension detection warnings, isolated autofill.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.AssumedThreats.PhysicalSeizureRisk
member.summary: Assume device may be physically seized or stolen. Enables: emergency wipe, decoy vaults, rapid lockout.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.TrustBoundaries
member.summary: Explicit trust boundaries for the vault. Defines what is considered inside vs outside the security perimeter.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.TrustBoundaries.TrustOnlyVaultApp
member.summary: The vault application itself is the only trusted component.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.TrustBoundaries.TrustOperatingSystem
member.summary: The operating system is NOT trusted.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.TrustBoundaries.TrustBrowser
member.summary: The browser is NOT trusted (for autofill purposes).

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.TrustBoundaries.TrustCloudServices
member.summary: Cloud services are NOT trusted.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.TrustBoundaries.TrustAiAssistants
member.summary: AI assistants are NOT trusted with secrets.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.TrustBoundaries.TrustUsbToken
member.summary: The USB hardware token is trusted when present.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.TrustBoundaries.TrustBiometrics
member.summary: Biometric systems are NOT trusted (spoofable).

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.VaultSecurityState
member.summary: Represents the overall security posture of a vault based on detected threats. Used by the Defence Engine to track vault compromise level.

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.VaultSecurityState.Normal
member.summary: Normal operation - no threats detected or all threats resolved.

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.VaultSecurityState.Suspicious
member.summary: Suspicious activity detected - vault remains functional but under increased scrutiny. May trigger additional authentication requirements or defensive measures.

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Models.Security.VaultSecurityState.Compromised
member.summary: Vault compromised - critical security breach detected. Vault should be locked, data scrubbed, and rekey required before normal operation resumes.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedCredential
member.summary: Encapsulates the data required to share a credential with another party. The credential itself is encrypted using a randomly generated symmetric key. That symmetric key is then encrypted with the recipient's public key (e.g. RSA‑4096). The payload can then be sent via email or other untrusted channels; only the recipient holding the corresponding private key can decrypt the symmetric key and thus access the credential. This mirrors the hybrid encryption scheme used elsewhere in the application.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedCredential.Title
member.summary: Identifier for the credential being shared. This might be used by the receiver to correlate with their own vault.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedCredential.EncryptedCredentialBase64
member.summary: The encrypted credential data. The plaintext consists of serialized information encrypted with a symmetric algorithm such as AES‑GCM.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedCredential.EncryptedKeyBase64
member.summary: The encrypted symmetric key, encoded in Base64. The sender encrypts the symmetric key using the recipient's public key (e.g. RSA‑4096). Only the recipient can decrypt this with their private key.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedCredential.CreatedUtc
member.summary: Timestamp indicating when this share package was created. May help receivers order or expire shared credentials.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry
member.summary: Represents a TOTP entry that can be synchronized between PhantomAttestor and PhantomObscura

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry.Id
member.summary: Unique identifier for this TOTP entry

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry.LinkedPasswordEntryId
member.summary: ID of the linked password entry in the vault

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry.Issuer
member.summary: Service issuer (e.g., "Google", "GitHub", "Microsoft")

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry.AccountName
member.summary: Account name or email (e.g., "user@example.com")

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry.Secret
member.summary: Base32-encoded secret key

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry.Digits
member.summary: Number of digits in the TOTP code (6 or 8)

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry.Period
member.summary: Time period in seconds (usually 30)

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry.Algorithm
member.summary: Hash algorithm (SHA1, SHA256, SHA512)

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry.LastModifiedUtc
member.summary: Last modification timestamp (UTC)

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedTotpEntry.ModifiedByApp
member.summary: Which application last modified this entry ("PhantomAttestor" or "PhantomObscura")

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest
member.summary: Represents metadata about an encrypted vault. This information is stored in the encrypted manifest file on the USB drive. None of the fields contain plaintext secrets; all sensitive values (such as the derived encryption key or password) are handled outside of this class.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.Version
member.summary: Version of the manifest schema. Increment this when breaking changes are made to the structure.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.VaultName
member.summary: Name of the vault (for display purposes only).

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.ContainerPath
member.summary: Full path to the encrypted container file on disk. The path is relative to the USB root to avoid leaking host information.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.UseVeraCrypt
member.summary: When true, the vault uses a VeraCrypt outer container and points to that container file. When false, the vault stores the encrypted database directly on disk and points to the inner zero-knowledge encrypted file (e.g., vault.pvault).

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.VeraCryptPasswordEncryptedBase64
member.summary: Auto-generated VeraCrypt password stored encrypted in the manifest. When the user doesn't provide a password during provisioning, the app generates a cryptographically secure random password for the VeraCrypt container, encrypts it with the manifest encryption key, and stores it here. This allows transparent VeraCrypt usage without requiring user password entry. The value is Base64-encoded encrypted data containing nonce, tag, and ciphertext.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.ContainerSizeBytes
member.summary: Size of the container in bytes. This field is recorded only to verify that the expected container exists; actual storage allocations are handled by the underlying encryption tool.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.CreatedUtc
member.summary: UTC timestamp when the vault was created. Using UTC avoids ambiguities around daylight savings or time zones.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.Description
member.summary: Optional description provided by the user. This may include a friendly name or purpose for the vault. Do not store secrets here.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.SaltBase64
member.summary: Salt used during key derivation. The salt must be stored in plaintext because it is needed to derive the same encryption key when unlocking the vault. It is not a secret but must be unique per vault.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.Algorithm
member.summary: Identifier of the encryption algorithm used (e.g. "AES-256-GCM"). This allows forward compatibility as algorithms evolve. When reading a manifest the application can select the appropriate decryption algorithm.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.KeyfilePath
member.summary: Optional path to a keyfile on disk. Keyfiles provide an additional entropy source and are required for multi-factor setups. If null, the vault relies solely on the passphrase.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.RequiresHardwareToken
member.summary: Flag indicating whether a hardware security device (e.g. YubiKey) must be present to unlock the vault. Applications should respect this and prompt the user accordingly.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.DeviceId
member.summary: Unique identifier of the removable drive that the vault is bound to. When provisioning a new vault this value is computed from properties of the selected USB device (for example, the volume serial number on Windows or a UUID on Linux). During unlock, the application verifies that the current drive matches this value to prevent copying the vault to another drive. Clearing or altering this field will break the binding and is not recommended.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.TotpSecret
member.summary: Base32‑encoded secret used for generating time‑based one‑time passwords (TOTP) as an additional factor during unlock. This property is optional and is only set when the user enables TOTP-based multi‑factor authentication. The secret itself is derived from a cryptographically secure random source and encoded using RFC 4648 Base32. Note that storing a TOTP secret alongside the manifest requires careful protection of the manifest; ensure that encryption and key derivation are sufficiently strong.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.RequiresTotp
member.summary: Indicates whether TOTP (Time-based One-Time Password) verification is required as an additional authentication factor when unlocking this vault. When true, users must provide a valid TOTP code from their authenticator app in addition to the password/keyfile.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.PasskeyId
member.summary: Public credential identifier for a registered FIDO2 passkey or WebAuthn credential. When using passkeys as a second factor, the authenticator returns an opaque credential ID which is stored here. During authentication, the application sends this ID to the authenticator to verify possession of the private key. This property is optional and may be null when passkeys are not in use.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.KemPublicKeyBase64
member.summary: Base64-encoded ML-KEM-768 (Kyber) public key for post-quantum hybrid encryption. This 1184-byte public key is used to encapsulate symmetric keys, providing protection against quantum computer attacks. The corresponding private key (2400 bytes) must be stored inside the encrypted container. When null, post-quantum encryption is not enabled for this vault.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.KemCiphertextBase64
member.summary: Base64-encoded ciphertext from KEM encapsulation of the vault's main symmetric key. This 1088-byte value is produced during vault provisioning when post-quantum encryption is enabled. To unlock the vault, the KEM private key is used to decapsulate this ciphertext and recover the symmetric key. When null, traditional key derivation (Argon2id) is used instead.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.KemPrivateKeyEncryptedBase64
member.summary: Base64-encoded encrypted ML-KEM-768 private key. The private key is encrypted with the manifest KEK (Argon2id-derived key) to protect it from unauthorized access. This allows storage in the manifest without creating circular dependencies. Stores a serialized EncryptionResult containing nonce, tag, and ciphertext. Size: 2400 bytes raw + ~100 bytes encryption overhead

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.YubiKeySerial
member.summary: Serial number of the YubiKey device bound to this vault. When set, the application will verify that the connected YubiKey matches this serial before allowing unlock operations. This provides device-level binding in addition to credential verification. Set to null to disable serial checking.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.YubiKeyCredentialIdBase64
member.summary: Base64-encoded FIDO2 credential ID for YubiKey authentication. This is returned during credential registration and must be presented during authentication challenges. Different from PasskeyId which is for platform authenticators.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.YubiKeyPublicKeyBase64
member.summary: Base64-encoded COSE public key for YubiKey FIDO2 signature verification. This public key is extracted during credential registration and used to verify authentication assertions. The key is encoded in COSE format per the WebAuthn specification.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.AutoFillEnabled
member.summary: Indicates whether automatic injection of credentials into websites or applications is enabled for this vault. When disabled, the application will not attempt to auto‑fill any fields regardless of other settings.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.AutoFillUsername
member.summary: Determines whether the username portion of a credential should be inserted automatically. This allows users to auto‑fill passwords while still typing usernames manually.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.AutoFillPassword
member.summary: Determines whether the password portion of a credential should be inserted automatically. Disabling this will require the user to type their password even when the vault auto‑fill is enabled.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.AutoBackupEnabled
member.summary: Enables or disables automatic creation of encrypted backup files each time the manifest or vault database is updated. When enabled, the application will write a backup copy of the manifest to a backups directory on the removable drive and prune older backups according to . Backups are encrypted using the same passphrase‑derived key as the primary manifest to ensure they remain confidential if copied or exfiltrated. This flag is false by default because automatic backups consume additional storage and may not be required in all scenarios.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.BackupRetentionDays
member.summary: Number of days to retain automatic backups before they are deleted. The backup service will remove any backup files older than this value. A default of 3 days provides a short window to recover from accidental deletion or corruption without accumulating excessive history. Users should adjust this according to their own recovery requirements. Values less than 1 disable pruning.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.DomainWhitelist
member.summary: Comma separated list of domain names for which auto‑fill is permitted. Domains should be specified without scheme. When empty, auto‑fill is allowed on all domains. This whitelist is intended to mitigate the risk of credential injection on malicious sites.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.FailedAttempts
member.summary: Tracks the number of consecutive failed unlock attempts. This counter is incremented each time an incorrect passphrase or multi‑factor token is provided. When the count reaches , the vault will either enforce a cooldown period (see ) or destroy itself if is true. Reset this value to zero after a successful unlock.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.MaxFailedAttempts
member.summary: Maximum number of consecutive failed attempts allowed before triggering a lockout or self‑destruct. A sensible default of 5 provides some tolerance for mistyped passwords while still protecting against brute‑force attacks. Users in high‑security environments can lower this value.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.SelfDestructEnabled
member.summary: When true the vault will permanently wipe its manifest and container when the failed attempt counter reaches . This mode should only be enabled by advanced users who understand the risks: any accidental mistype could irrevocably destroy the data. When false the vault will simply lock for a cooldown period.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.LockedUntilUtc
member.summary: If set, indicates that the vault is currently locked out due to repeated failed attempts. The value stores the UTC time until which unlock attempts should be refused. Applications must compare the current UTC time to this property and disallow unlocking until the cooldown has expired. Once the vault is successfully unlocked the property should be cleared.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.Categories
member.summary: Ordered list of categories for organizing credentials. This property was added to allow the UI to persist category metadata alongside the rest of the manifest. It may be null for older manifests and should be treated as optional by callers.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.SecurityState
member.summary: Overall security posture of the vault based on Defence Engine threat detection. Tracked states: Normal (no threats), Suspicious (warnings detected), Compromised (critical breach). Defaults to Normal for backwards compatibility.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.RekeyRequired
member.summary: When true, the vault requires a full rekey operation before normal use can resume. Set by the Defence Engine when a compromised state is detected or when security policy mandates regeneration of all encryption keys. The user must provide current credentials, generate new keys, and re-encrypt the vault database.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.TrustedDevices
member.summary: List of device fingerprints that have been explicitly trusted for this vault. When a vault is opened from a new device, the Defence Engine raises a NewDeviceFingerprint threat. Users can choose to trust the device, adding its fingerprint to this list. Subsequent access from trusted devices will not trigger warnings. Defaults to empty list for backwards compatibility.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.LastKeyRotation
member.summary: Rotation metadata to help the client prompt for periodic rekey.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.IntegritySignatureBase64
member.summary: Optional HMAC-SHA256 signature of critical manifest fields for additional integrity verification beyond GCM authentication. When set, the signature is computed over a canonical representation of: ContainerPath, SaltBase64, DeviceId, KemPublicKeyBase64, and YubiKeySerial. The signature key is derived from the manifest KEK. This provides defense-in-depth against manifest tampering even if an attacker obtains the KEK.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.SignatureTimestamp
member.summary: Timestamp when the integrity signature was last updated. Used to detect stale signatures and determine if re-signing is needed after manifest changes.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.PinSaltBase64
member.summary: Base64-encoded salt for PIN lock key derivation. When set, this vault has a PIN lock configured. The PIN is hashed with PBKDF2 using this salt. This allows per-vault PIN configuration independent of global settings.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.PinHashBase64
member.summary: Base64-encoded PBKDF2-SHA256 hash of the vault's PIN. Used to verify PIN entry during soft lock unlock. The hash is computed with 150,000 iterations using the salt stored in PinSaltBase64.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.PinPbkdf2Iterations
member.summary: Number of PBKDF2 iterations used for PIN hashing. Defaults to 150,000 but can be increased for stronger protection against brute force attacks.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.RecoveryCodes
member.summary: Array of recovery code metadata for account recovery. Recovery codes provide a fallback authentication method when all other factors are lost. Each code is single-use and cryptographically hashed for secure storage. Users should print these codes and store them securely offline.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.BackupSigningPublicKeyBase64
member.summary: Base64-encoded ECDSA P-256 public key for backup signature verification. Used to verify the integrity and authenticity of backup files. Generated once during vault creation and stored in the manifest.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.EncryptedBackupSigningKeyBase64
member.summary: Base64-encoded encrypted ECDSA P-256 private key for backup signing. Encrypted with the vault passphrase using AES-256-GCM. The private key is needed to create signed backups.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.ThreatModel
member.summary: Explicit threat model metadata for hostile-environment vault operation. Makes security assumptions first-class for UI warnings, AI auditing, and compliance. Designed for zero-trust environments where no component is trusted by default.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.AiAccessPolicy
member.summary: AI-aware access policy that actively defends against AI leakage. Blocks clipboard sharing to AI contexts, prevents autofill to AI chat interfaces, and supports AI-safe view mode with masked secrets.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.SupplyChainEvidence
member.summary: Supply chain evidence capture for forensic analysis. Stores hashes of tools that accessed the vault, optional known-good fingerprint, and access history. No scanning or monitoring - just evidence capture.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest.SecurityCapabilities
member.summary: Security capabilities manifest for compliance documentation. Exposes provable design capabilities for audit responses, investor decks, and enterprise pilots. No dashboards - just provable design.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.BackupService
member.summary: Provides functionality for creating and pruning encrypted backup copies of the vault manifest. Backups are encrypted with a fresh random salt and nonce on each invocation and stored as self‑contained JSON payloads. The retention period for backups is configurable via the settings. This service does not persist any secrets on disk outside the backup files themselves; it relies on the caller to supply the passphrase when creating a backup.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.BackupService.CreateBackupAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an encrypted backup of the specified manifest file. The output file is written to the provided backup directory with a timestamped filename. If the backup directory does not exist, it will be created. The backup payload contains the salt, nonce, tag and ciphertext encoded in Base64 for ease of parsing and transport.
member.param: Absolute path to the manifest file.
User passphrase used to derive the encryption key. May be null when a keyfile is provided.
Optional path to a keyfile; contents are appended to the passphrase for key derivation.
Directory where backups should be stored.
member.returns: The full path to the created backup file.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.BackupService.RestoreBackupAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Restores an encrypted manifest backup created by . The backup payload is decrypted using the supplied passphrase and optional keyfile and written to the target manifest path, overwriting any existing file.
member.param: Path to the encrypted backup file.
Destination path for the restored manifest.
User passphrase used when the backup was created. May be null when a keyfile is provided.
Optional path to a keyfile whose contents were combined with the passphrase.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.BackupService.PruneBackups(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes backup files older than the specified retention period from the backup directory. If the directory does not exist or contains files that cannot be parsed for timestamps, those files are ignored. This method is synchronous and should not be called on a UI thread if the directory contains many files.
member.param: Number of days to retain backups. Files older than this are deleted.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.BackupService.GenerateSigningKeyPair
member.summary: Generates an ECDSA P-256 key pair for backup signature verification. This should be called once during vault creation.
member.returns: Tuple of (privateKey, publicKey) in DER format

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.BackupService.CreateSignedBackupAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Creates a signed backup by appending an ECDSA signature to the backup file. The signature is computed over the entire backup payload and appended with its length.
member.param: Path to the manifest file to backup
ECDSA private key in DER format
member.returns: Path to the signed backup file

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.BackupService.VerifyBackupSignature(System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Verifies the ECDSA signature of a signed backup file.
member.param: Path to the signed backup file
ECDSA public key in SubjectPublicKeyInfo format
member.returns: True if signature is valid; false otherwise

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.BackupService.RestoreSignedBackupAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Restores a signed backup after verifying its signature. Throws if signature verification fails.
member.param: Path to the signed backup file

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.DomainKeyProvider
member.summary:
Provides cryptographically isolated domain keys using HKDF derivation.
Security model: - Master key derived from password + salt via Argon2id (external) - Master key passed to Initialize(), domain keys derived, master zeroed - Each domain gets: HKDF-SHA256(master, salt, "phantom.{domain}.v1") - Domain keys are memory-locked and zeroed on disposal/lock
This is the foundation for domain separation. Even without process isolation, services can only access their designated domain key.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.DomainKeyProvider.Initialize(System.Byte[],System.Byte[])
member.summary: Initializes the provider by deriving domain keys from the master key. The master key is zeroed immediately after derivation.
member.param: Master key from Argon2id (will be zeroed)

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.DomainKeyProvider.DeriveDomainKey(System.Byte[],System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Derives a domain-specific key using HKDF-SHA256.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.DomainKeyProvider.TryLockMemory(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Attempts to lock memory pages to prevent swapping to disk. Best effort - may fail on some platforms.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.DomainKeyProvider.ZeroAndFreeKey(System.Byte[]@,System.Runtime.InteropServices.GCHandle@)
member.summary: Zeros a key buffer and frees its GC handle.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.DomainStorageService
member.summary:
Service for reading/writing domain-specific encrypted stores.
Each domain has its own encrypted store file: - obscura.store.encrypted → encrypted with K_obscura - attestor.store.encrypted → encrypted with K_attestor - recovery.store.encrypted → encrypted with K_recovery
Storage separation ensures: - Different keys for each domain - Different files (can't accidentally mix) - Different parsers (type safety)
This makes later process isolation trivial since each process only needs its own domain key.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.DomainStorageService.LoadObscuraStoreAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Loads the Obscura store from the specified USB path.

member.summary: Loads the Attestor store from the specified USB path.

member.summary: Saves the Attestor store to the specified USB path.

member.summary: Loads the Recovery store from the specified USB path.

member.summary: Checks if individual stores exist.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.IDomainKeyProvider
member.summary:
Provides cryptographically isolated domain keys derived from a master key.
Architecture: - Master key is derived from password via Argon2id (happens once at unlock) - Domain keys are derived via HKDF with domain-specific labels - Master key is zeroed immediately after domain key derivation - Each domain only receives its own key - never the master
This creates cryptographic domain separation even within a single process. Later, process isolation can enforce this at the OS level.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.IDomainKeyProvider.IsUnlocked
member.summary: Whether the provider has been initialized with domain keys.

member.summary: Gets the Obscura domain key for vault/credential storage operations. Throws if not unlocked.
member.returns: 32-byte domain key (caller must NOT zero this - managed by provider)

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.IDomainKeyProvider.GetAttestorKey
member.summary: Gets the Attestor domain key for identity/authentication operations. Throws if not unlocked.
member.returns: 32-byte domain key (caller must NOT zero this - managed by provider)

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.IDomainKeyProvider.GetRecoveryKey
member.summary: Gets the Recovery domain key for break-glass operations. Throws if not unlocked.
member.returns: 32-byte domain key (caller must NOT zero this - managed by provider)

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.IDomainKeyProvider.Lock
member.summary: Locks all domain keys, zeroing them from memory. Must be called on USB removal, session timeout, or explicit lock.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.RecoveryAuditService
member.summary:
Service for recovery audit logging and rekey trigger management.
Responsibilities: - Append hash-chained entries to recovery audit log - Track rekey requirements after recovery operations - Enforce recovery policy (delays, device binding, etc.) - Maintain audit chain integrity
CRITICAL: Every recovery operation MUST be logged. The audit chain is tamper-evident via hash linking.

member.summary: Event raised when a domain requires rekeying after recovery. Subscribers should initiate key rotation for the affected domain.
Logs an audit entry to the recovery store. Maintains hash chain integrity.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.RecoverySession
member.summary:
Manages a recovery session with policy enforcement.
Recovery session lifecycle: 1. Enter recovery mode (logs RecoveryModeEntered) 2. Validate recovery code (logs CodeValidationAttempt) 3. Wait for recovery delay (policy-configurable) 4. Select domain to recover (explicit selection required) 5. Unseal domain recovery key (logs DomainRecovered) 6. Trigger rekey (logs KeyRotationTriggered) 7. Exit recovery mode (logs RecoveryModeExited)
Session auto-expires after MaxSessionSeconds. USB removal during recovery terminates session immediately.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.RecoverySession.State
member.summary: Current session state.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.RecoverySession.RequiresUsbPresence

member.summary: Recovery delay in seconds (from policy).

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.RecoverySession.SessionSecondsRemaining
member.summary: Time remaining in session (seconds).

member.summary: Remaining unused recovery codes.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.ScopedRecoveryService
member.summary:
Service for scoped recovery operations.
Recovery flow: 1. User enters recovery code → validates against stored hash 2. Recovery code derives K_recovery_session via HKDF 3. User explicitly selects domain to recover (Obscura OR Attestor) 4. K_recovery_session decrypts the sealed blob for chosen domain 5. Recovered domain key is used to rewrap/rekey domain store 6. Mandatory key rotation + invalidate used recovery codes
CRITICAL: Recovery does NOT unlock the master key. It only unseals ONE domain at a time.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.ScopedRecoveryService.GenerateDomainRecoveryKey
member.summary: Generates a new random recovery key for a domain. This key can restore access to the domain if the main password is lost.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.ScopedRecoveryService.SealDomainRecoveryKey(System.Byte[],System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},PhantomVault.Core.Models.DomainStores.CryptoDomain)
member.summary: Seals a domain recovery key using the recovery domain key. The sealed blob can only be opened with K_recovery.
member.param: The recovery key for the domain (e.g., K_obscura_recover)
member.returns: Sealed recovery key blob

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.DomainKeys.ScopedRecoveryService.UnsealDomainRecoveryKey(PhantomVault.Core.Models.DomainStores.SealedRecoveryKey,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},PhantomVault.Core.Models.DomainStores.CryptoDomain)
member.summary: Unseals a domain recovery key. Call this during recovery mode to restore access to a domain.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionResult
member.summary: Represents the output of an encryption operation. The caller is responsible for persisting the values in a secure format. None of the properties should be altered before decryption.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionResult.Ciphertext
member.summary: The encrypted data. This includes any padding added by the encryption algorithm. Do not truncate or otherwise modify.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionResult.Nonce
member.summary: The unique nonce used for the encryption. This must be passed unchanged into the decryption routine. A fresh nonce must be generated for every encryption operation.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionResult.Tag
member.summary: The authentication tag produced by authenticated encryption algorithms such as AES‑GCM. The tag ensures integrity and authenticity of the ciphertext and associated data.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionService
member.summary: Provides symmetric encryption using a memory‑hard key derivation algorithm (Argon2id) and authenticated encryption (AES‑GCM). This service never stores derived keys and attempts to zero sensitive material when it is no longer needed. It is thread‑safe but does perform allocations for each operation.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionService.GenerateSalt(System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a cryptographically secure random salt of the given length in bytes. Salts need not be secret but must be unique per derived key to avoid rainbow table attacks.
member.param: Number of random bytes to generate.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionService.DeriveKey(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char},System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Derives a fixed‑length key from a passphrase and salt using Argon2id. This implementation follows OWASP recommendations for password hashing by allocating a large amount of memory and performing multiple iterations. Should the Konscious Argon2 package be unavailable (for example on an unsupported platform), the method falls back to PBKDF2 with HMAC‑SHA256. Although PBKDF2 offers weaker GPU/ASIC resistance than Argon2id, it remains a FIPS‑approved option when run with a high work factor. These defaults can be tuned via optional parameters.
member.param: The unique salt associated with this key.
The desired key size in bytes.
member.returns: A byte array containing the derived key.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionService.Encrypt(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Encrypts arbitrary plaintext using AES‑256‑GCM. The method generates a fresh 96‑bit nonce for each call. The returned contains the ciphertext, nonce and authentication tag. Optional associated data may be supplied to bind contextual information (such as file names) to the ciphertext.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionService.Decrypt(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Decrypts ciphertext produced by . The same key, nonce, tag and associated data used to encrypt the data must be supplied. If any value is incorrect, an exception will be thrown.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IHybridEncryptionService
member.summary: Abstraction for the post-quantum hybrid encryption workflow used during vault provisioning. Implementations must encapsulate/decrypt secrets using a Kyber (ML-KEM) key pair and then feed the resulting shared secret into the symmetric helpers.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IHybridEncryptionService.GenerateKeyPair
member.summary: Generates a Kyber key pair.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IHybridEncryptionService.EncapsulateSecret(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Encapsulates a shared secret with the provided public key.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IHybridEncryptionService.DecapsulateSecret(System.Byte[],System.Byte[])
member.summary: Decapsulates the shared secret using the private key.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IHybridEncryptionService.EncryptWithHybrid(System.Byte[],System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Encrypts plaintext with AES-GCM using a fresh Kyber-derived shared secret. Returns the KEM ciphertext alongside the AEAD payload.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IHybridEncryptionService.DecryptWithHybrid(System.Byte[],PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionResult,System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Decrypts a hybrid payload by decapsulating the shared secret and then decrypting the AES-GCM ciphertext.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.HybridEncryptionService
member.summary: Production implementation of combining AES-GCM with ML-KEM-768 (CRYSTALS-Kyber) for post-quantum security.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.HybridEncryptionService.GenerateKeyPair
member.summary: Generates a new ML-KEM-768 key pair for post-quantum encryption. ML-KEM-768 provides security equivalent to AES-192 against quantum attacks. The public key ( bytes) should be stored in the manifest, while the private key ( bytes) must be stored inside the encrypted container.
member.returns: Tuple of (publicKey, privateKey) as byte arrays.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.HybridEncryptionService.EncapsulateSecret(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Encapsulates a shared secret using the recipient's ML-KEM public key. This produces a ciphertext ( bytes for Kyber768) that can only be decrypted by the holder of the corresponding private key. The shared secret ( bytes) is used as the AES-GCM key for data encryption.
member.param: The recipient's ML-KEM-768 public key ( bytes).
member.returns: Tuple of (ciphertext, sharedSecret).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.HybridEncryptionService.DecapsulateSecret(System.Byte[],System.Byte[])
member.summary: Decapsulates the shared secret from a KEM ciphertext using the private key. This recovers the -byte symmetric key needed to decrypt the data payload.
member.param: The KEM ciphertext ( bytes) produced during encapsulation.
member.returns: The shared secret ( bytes).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.HybridEncryptionService.EncryptWithHybrid(System.Byte[],System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Encrypts arbitrary plaintext using post-quantum hybrid encryption. A new shared secret is encapsulated with the provided public key and used as the AES-256-GCM key. The caller must store both the KEM ciphertext and the encrypted data together.
member.returns: Tuple of (kemCiphertext, encryptedData).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.HybridEncryptionService.DecryptWithHybrid(System.Byte[],PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionResult,System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Decrypts hybrid-encrypted data using the private key. The shared secret is first recovered via KEM decapsulation, then used to decrypt the data payload with AES-256-GCM.
member.param: The KEM ciphertext containing the encapsulated key.
The associated authenticated data used during encryption.
member.returns: The decrypted plaintext.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IntrusionService
member.summary: Manages intrusion detection and response for the vault. Tracks failed unlock attempts, enforces cooldown periods and performs self‑destruct when enabled. This service operates on the encrypted manifest; callers must supply the correct passphrase when writing changes.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IntrusionService.RegisterFailedAttempt(PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Should be called after an unsuccessful unlock attempt. It increments the failed attempt counter and either enforces a cooldown or triggers self‑destruct when thresholds are reached. If a cooldown is applied the manifest is updated.
member.param: Absolute path to the encrypted manifest file.
Root of the removable drive, needed for self‑destruct.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IntrusionService.ResetAttempts(PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Resets the failed attempt counter and clears any lockout on successful unlock. The updated manifest is persisted.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IntrusionService.SelfDestruct(PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Performs irreversible destruction of the vault container and manifest. This method should be used sparingly and only when self‑destruct is enabled and the failed attempt threshold has been reached. After wiping, the files cannot be recovered.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.IntrusionService.SecureDelete(System.String)

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.LayeredEncryptionService
member.summary: Provides layered encryption with configurable depth for defense-in-depth security. Supports 2, 3, and 5-layer encryption schemes using multiple algorithms: - Layer 1: AES-256-GCM (authenticated encryption with integrity) - Layer 2: ChaCha20-Poly1305 (stream cipher with authentication) - Layer 3: AES-256-CBC (traditional block cipher) - Layer 4: Twofish-256 (additional algorithm diversity) - Layer 5: Serpent-256 (final layer for maximum security)

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.LayeredEncryptionService.SecurityLevel
member.summary: Encryption security levels matching different data sensitivity tiers.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.ManifestService
member.summary:
Handles persisting and retrieving encrypted manifest files. A manifest stores metadata about the vault (for example the container size and configuration) without revealing any secrets. The manifest is authenticated and encrypted with a passphrase‑derived key (KEK).
Phase 2: The manifest itself remains encrypted with traditional Argon2id. However, it now stores the encrypted ML-KEM private key and KEM ciphertext for post-quantum vault data encryption.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.ManifestService.WriteManifest(PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes an encrypted manifest to disk. The manifest itself is serialized to JSON and then encrypted using a key derived from the provided passphrase and the generated salt. If a keyfile path is provided, its contents are concatenated with the passphrase prior to derivation.
member.param: Optional USB serial number for additional AAD binding.
When true, both passphrase AND keyfile are required (dual-factor authentication).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.ManifestService.WriteManifestSecure(PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest,System.String,PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePassword,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Writes an encrypted manifest to disk using secure password handling. This method ensures that passwords are properly zeroed from memory after use. RECOMMENDED: Use this method instead of the string-based overload for better security.
member.param: The user passphrase (will be securely zeroed after use).
Optional USB serial number for additional AAD binding.
When true, both passphrase AND keyfile are required (dual-factor authentication).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.ManifestService.ReadManifest(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Reads an encrypted manifest from disk and returns the deserialized . The caller must supply the correct passphrase and optional keyfile path. If the file is corrupted or the authentication fails, an exception is thrown.
Phase 2: Manifest is always decrypted with traditional KEK, not hybrid. The manifest contains encrypted KEM private key and KEM ciphertext that are used for vault data encryption.
member.param: Path to the manifest on disk.
Optional USB serial number for AAD validation.
When true, both passphrase AND keyfile are required (dual-factor authentication).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.ManifestService.TryReadManifest(System.String,System.String,System.String,PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest@,System.String@,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Non-throwing variant of ReadManifest. Attempts to read and decrypt the manifest. On failure returns false and a human-friendly error message suitable for UI display.
Phase 2: Manifest is always decrypted with traditional KEK.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.ManifestService.ComputeIntegritySignature(PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Computes an HMAC-SHA256 signature over critical manifest fields. The signature provides defense-in-depth integrity verification.
member.returns: Base64-encoded HMAC signature.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.ManifestService.VerifyIntegritySignature(PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest,System.Byte[],System.Boolean)
member.summary: Verifies the integrity signature of a manifest. Returns true if the signature is valid or if no signature is present (for backwards compatibility).
member.param: When true, throws if no signature is present.
member.returns: True if signature is valid or absent; throws SecurityException otherwise.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.ManifestService.SignManifest(PhantomVault.Core.Models.VaultManifest,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Signs the manifest by computing and storing an HMAC-SHA256 signature over critical fields. Call this before serializing the manifest.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.MerkleAuditService
member.summary: Provides helper methods for computing a Merkle root of an audit log. A Merkle tree allows all entries in the log to be summarised by a single root hash. If any entry changes, the root will be different. In practice the root can be stored alongside the log or anchored to an external timestamping service for additional assurance.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.MerkleAuditService.ComputeMerkleRoot(System.String)
member.summary: Reads all audit entries from the given file and computes a Merkle root hash. The audit file must contain one JSON record per line matching the schema. If the file does not exist or is empty, this method returns null.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.MigrationService
member.summary:
Provides facilities for migrating a vault manifest between encryption algorithms. As cryptography evolves over time and new standards emerge (such as XChaCha20 or post‑quantum hybrids), existing vaults may need to be re‑encrypted with a different algorithm. The migration service reads the current manifest using the provided passphrase and keyfile, updates its algorithm identifier and re‑writes it using .
Note: In this simplified implementation, only the manifest encryption algorithm is updated. Migrating the underlying container (e.g. a VeraCrypt volume) would require creating a new container and transferring its contents, which is outside the scope of this service.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.MigrationService.MigrateManifest(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Migrates the manifest at the specified path to a new encryption algorithm. If the manifest already uses the requested algorithm, no action is taken. Otherwise the algorithm field is updated and the manifest is re‑written encrypted under the existing passphrase/keyfile.
member.param: Absolute path to the encrypted manifest.
Passphrase used to decrypt the manifest.
Optional keyfile path if used during provisioning.
The name of the new encryption algorithm (e.g. "XChaCha20-Poly1305").

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.PasskeyService
member.summary: Provides platform authenticator support for registering and validating passkeys. On Windows this bridges to Windows Hello via the UserConsentVerifier API. member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.PasswordHealthService
member.summary: Analyses a collection of credentials and produces a health report. Metrics include password entropy, reuse and age. The service optionally integrates with Have I Been Pwned (HIBP) using the k‑anonymous API to flag passwords found in known breaches. A Bloom filter can be provided to avoid network calls and improve privacy. To use the breach check, set to true and supply a delegate that performs the k‑anon lookup.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.PasswordHealthService.HibpLookupAsync
member.summary: Delegate type for performing a k‑anonymous HIBP lookup. It receives the first five hexadecimal characters of a SHA1 password hash and returns the number of matches found. The lookup should not send the full hash to maintain privacy.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.PasswordHealthService.AnalyzeAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{PhantomVault.Core.Models.Credential},System.Double,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Performs analysis on the provided credentials. Weakness thresholds can be adjusted via parameters. Breach checking is only performed if enabled and a lookup delegate is provided.
member.param: Consider passwords reused if they appear at least this many times.
Consider passwords old if they have not been updated in this number of days.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.PasswordHealthService.ComputeEntropy(System.String)
member.summary: Computes an approximate Shannon entropy of a password. The entropy is in bits and assumes independence of characters.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.PasswordHealthService.IsBreachedAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Determines whether a password appears in known breach data using the k‑anonymous model. The default implementation returns false because no breach lookup delegate has been provided.

member.summary: Standard secure erasure (3 passes)

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Services.SecureDeletionService.DeletionMethod.SimpleOverwrite
member.summary: Simple single pass overwrite

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.AdvancedDebuggerDetection
member.summary: Advanced debugger detection techniques beyond basic IsDebuggerPresent checks. SECURITY: Implements multiple anti-debugging methods for defense in depth.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.AdvancedDebuggerDetection.IsDebuggerAttached
member.summary: Performs comprehensive debugger detection using multiple techniques.
member.returns: True if any debugger detection method succeeds

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.AdvancedDebuggerDetection.IsRemoteDebuggerPresent
member.summary: Checks if a remote debugger is attached (kernel debugger or remote debugging).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.AdvancedDebuggerDetection.CheckPEBBeingDebugged
member.summary: Checks the PEB (Process Environment Block) BeingDebugged flag directly. This is more reliable than IsDebuggerPresent as it checks the raw structure.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.AdvancedDebuggerDetection.DetectDebugPort
member.summary: Checks for debug port using NtQueryInformationProcess. A debugger attaches to a debug port which can be detected.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.AdvancedDebuggerDetection.TimingCheckDetection
member.summary: Uses timing to detect if execution is being slowed by a debugger. Debuggers introduce significant timing delays during instruction stepping.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.AdvancedDebuggerDetection.DetectDebuggerDLLs
member.summary: Checks for common debugger DLLs loaded in the process.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.AdvancedDebuggerDetection.CheckHardwareBreakpoints
member.summary: Checks for debugger by attempting to read debug registers. Hardware breakpoints are stored in debug registers DR0-DR7.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.AdvancedDebuggerDetection.QuickDebuggerCheck
member.summary: Performs a quick debugger check suitable for frequent polling. Only uses fast methods to minimize performance impact.

member.summary: Provides functionality to exclude clipboard data from Windows Clipboard History. Windows 10 version 1809+ stores clipboard history which can expose sensitive data. This service uses special clipboard formats to prevent history storage.

member.summary: Checks if clipboard history exclusion is supported on this platform. Only supported on Windows 10 version 1809 and later.

member.summary: Copies text to clipboard while excluding it from Windows Clipboard History. The data will still be available for immediate paste but won't appear in clipboard history (Win+V) or be synced across devices.
member.param: The text to copy to clipboard.
member.returns: True if the operation succeeded.

member.summary: Copies text to clipboard with exclusion and schedules automatic clearing.

member.returns: True if the initial copy succeeded.

member.summary: Clears the clipboard contents.

member.summary: Processes a threat event and executes matching defensive rules.

member.summary: Minimum threat level required to activate this rule.

member.summary: Default implementation of IDefenceSettingsService that persists rule enable/disable state to a JSON configuration file in the user's local application data folder.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.DeviceFingerprintProvider
member.summary: Generates stable device fingerprints by combining hardware, OS, and user context. Uses a persistent application-specific ID to ensure consistency across sessions. All operations remain offline (no network calls or telemetry).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.DeviceFingerprintProvider.GetCurrentFingerprint
member.summary: Generates a fingerprint for the current device and user context. Creates or loads a persistent device ID from user's AppData for consistency.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.DeviceFingerprintProvider.GetOrCreateMachineId
member.summary: Gets or creates a stable machine identifier stored in user's AppData. This ensures the same device always produces the same MachineId even if hardware changes slightly (e.g., RAM upgrade). For maximum security, consider hashing additional hardware identifiers (CPU serial, disk ID).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.DeviceFingerprintProvider.GenerateStableMachineId
member.summary: Generates a stable machine identifier by hashing environment properties. Combines machine name, OS version, user name, and processor count. For production, consider adding hardware serial numbers if accessible.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.DeviceFingerprintProvider.GetOsFamily
member.summary: Determines OS family from platform ID for cross-platform identification.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.ExportGuard
member.summary: Implements export throttling with threat detection. Tracks export events in a 1-hour sliding window and enforces cooldown when threshold exceeded.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.ExportGuard.CanExport(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if export is allowed (not in cooldown).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.ExportGuard.RegisterExport(System.String)
member.summary: Registers an export event and checks for excessive exporting.

member.summary: Adds an artificial delay to the authentication process. Used to slow down brute-force attacks.

member.summary: Locks the vault for a temporary period, preventing any unlock attempts.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.IAuthController.RequireReauthentication(System.String)
member.summary: Forces re-authentication with a reason displayed to the user. Used when security threats are detected.

member.summary: Checks if a clipboard copy operation is allowed. Returns false if the guard is in cooldown due to excessive copying.

True to enable the rule, false to disable it.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.IDeviceFingerprintProvider
member.summary: Generates stable device fingerprints for detecting vault access from new/unknown devices. Fingerprints are based on hardware, OS, and user context to provide consistent identification while remaining offline (no network-based identifiers).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.IDeviceFingerprintProvider.GetCurrentFingerprint
member.summary: Generates a fingerprint for the current device and user context. The fingerprint includes machine ID, OS family/version, hostname, and username.
member.returns: A DeviceFingerprint representing the current device.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.IExportGuard
member.summary: Guards vault export operations to detect and prevent data exfiltration. Tracks export events in a sliding window and raises threats when thresholds are exceeded.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.IExportGuard.CanExport(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if an export operation is allowed for the specified export type. Returns false if the guard is in cooldown due to excessive exports.
member.returns: True if export is allowed, false if blocked by cooldown.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.IExportGuard.RegisterExport(System.String)
member.summary: Registers an export event for threat detection. Call this after successfully completing an export operation.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.IncidentState
member.summary: Tracks cumulative security incident score with time decay. Used by Defence Engine to determine vault security state.

member.summary: Cumulative incident score from threat events. Decays exponentially over time (default 30 minutes).

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.IncidentState.State
member.summary: Current vault security state based on score thresholds. Normal (less than 4), Suspicious (4 or greater), Compromised (10 or greater).

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Services.Security.IncidentState.LastUpdated
member.summary: Timestamp of last incident score update. Used to calculate time decay when new threats occur.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.SharingService
member.summary: Implements a hybrid encryption scheme for securely sharing individual credentials or folders with trusted contacts. A randomly generated symmetric key is used to encrypt the credential payload with AES‑256‑GCM. That symmetric key is then encrypted with the recipient's RSA‑4096 public key. This method ensures that only the intended recipient can decrypt the symmetric key and access the shared data.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.SharingService.CreateShareAsync(PhantomVault.Core.Models.Credential,System.Security.Cryptography.RSAParameters)
member.summary: Creates a share package for the given credential. The caller must provide the recipient's RSA public key parameters. The resulting can be serialized and transmitted to the recipient over an untrusted channel.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.SharingService.DecryptShare(PhantomVault.Core.Models.SharedCredential,System.Security.Cryptography.RSAParameters)
member.summary: Decrypts a share package using the recipient's private RSA key. Returns the original credential. The caller must ensure they control the corresponding private key.

The name of the process (for logging).

member.summary: Specifies the HMAC algorithm used for TOTP generation. RFC 6238 defaults to SHA1 but allows SHA256 and SHA512 for higher security.

member.name: F:PhantomVault.Core.Services.TotpAlgorithm.SHA1
member.summary: HMAC-SHA1 (RFC 6238 default, 160-bit digest).

member.summary: HMAC-SHA256 (256-bit digest, recommended for new implementations).

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.TotpService
member.summary: Implements RFC 6238 Time-based One-Time Password (TOTP) generation. Given a shared secret and the current time, this service produces a numeric code valid for a short time window (usually 30 seconds). TOTP is commonly used as a second factor in multi-factor authentication. Supports SHA1 (default), SHA256, and SHA512 hash algorithms.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.TotpService.GenerateSecret(System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a new random secret suitable for TOTP. The secret consists of the specified number of bytes and is returned as a base32-encoded string without padding.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.TotpService.GenerateCode(System.String,System.Nullable{System.DateTimeOffset},System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a TOTP code from a base32-encoded secret using HMAC-SHA1 (default).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.TotpService.GenerateCode(System.String,PhantomVault.Core.Services.TotpAlgorithm,System.Nullable{System.DateTimeOffset},System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a TOTP code from a base32-encoded secret using the specified HMAC algorithm. SHA256 and SHA512 provide stronger security than the default SHA1.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UnlockThrottleService
member.summary: Provides unlock attempt throttling that works independently of manifest decryption. Stores failed attempts in a DPAPI-protected local file, keyed by manifest file hash. This allows throttling even when the wrong password is entered.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbBindingService
member.summary:
Responsible for computing a stable identifier for a removable drive and verifying that it matches a previously recorded identifier. This is used to bind a vault to a specific USB device so it cannot simply be copied onto another drive. The implementation is cross‑platform: on Windows it uses the volume serial number via a native API, and on Unix-like systems it derives an identifier from file system properties.
For higher assurance, this service also supports a hidden per-device identifier file (.phantom_device_id) that contains a cryptographically random ID with an HMAC signature. This provides stronger protection against drive cloning since the hidden file cannot be easily replicated.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbBindingService.DeriveHmacKeyFromVaultSalt(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Derives a per-vault HMAC key from the vault's salt. This ensures each vault has a unique HMAC key that cannot be reused across vaults. Uses HKDF (HMAC-based Key Derivation Function) as specified in RFC 5869.
member.returns: A 32-byte HMAC key derived from the vault salt.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbBindingService.ComputeDeviceId(System.String)
member.summary: Computes a unique identifier for the given drive root. On Windows this uses the volume serial number returned by GetVolumeInformation. On non‑Windows platforms the method combines the drive's total capacity, file system format and volume label and hashes the result with SHA‑256. The returned value is hex encoded.
member.param: The root path of the removable drive (e.g. "E:\\" or "/media/user/USB").

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbBindingService.EnsureBoundToDevice(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verifies that the removable drive at the given root has the expected device identifier. If the IDs do not match an exception is thrown.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbBindingService.CreateHiddenDeviceId(System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Creates a hidden device identifier file on the drive for high-assurance binding. This file contains a cryptographically random ID with an HMAC signature that cannot be easily replicated when cloning a drive.
member.param: The root path of the removable drive.
The vault's unique salt for deriving the HMAC key.
member.returns: The device ID stored in the hidden file.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbBindingService.ReadHiddenDeviceId(System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Reads and validates the hidden device identifier from the drive. Throws if the file is missing, corrupted, or has an invalid signature.
member.param: The root path of the removable drive.
The vault's unique salt for deriving the HMAC key.
member.returns: The validated device ID.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbBindingService.HasHiddenDeviceId(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if a hidden device identifier file exists on the drive.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbBindingService.ComputeHighAssuranceDeviceId(System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Computes a combined device ID using both the volume serial and hidden file. This provides the strongest binding guarantee.
member.param: The root path of the removable drive.
The vault's unique salt for deriving the HMAC key.
member.returns: Combined device ID (volume + hidden).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbBindingService.InitializeHighAssuranceBinding(System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Initializes high-assurance binding on a new drive. Creates the hidden ID file and returns the combined device ID.
member.param: The root path of the removable drive.
The vault's unique salt for deriving the HMAC key.
member.returns: Combined device ID (volume + hidden).

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbDetector
member.summary: Desktop implementation of USB drive detection for Windows, macOS, and Linux. This service abstracts platform differences and exposes a simple enumeration API. For Linux and macOS it scans common mount points; for Windows it inspects entries. Consumers may subscribe to the and events to be notified of changes.

member.name: E:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbDetector.RemovableDriveInserted
member.summary: Raised when a new removable drive is detected. The argument contains the root path of the drive (e.g. "E:\\" on Windows or "/media/username/USB" on Linux).

member.name: E:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbDetector.RemovableDriveRemoved
member.summary: Raised when a removable drive is removed.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbDetector.GetRemovableDrives
member.summary: Enumerates all currently connected removable drives. This method returns a snapshot and does not monitor for changes. Use the events for real‑time updates.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbDetector.IsValidRemovableDrive(System.String)
member.summary: Checks if a given path represents a valid, currently-mounted removable drive.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.UsbDetector.GetDriveInfo(System.String)
member.summary: Gets detailed information about a removable drive. Returns null if the drive is not accessible.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.VeraCryptService
member.summary: Minimal VeraCrypt CLI wrapper implementing IVeraCryptService. Writes password to stdin, captures stdout/stderr, and returns a VeraCryptResult. Includes timeout logic, exit code mapping, and retry mechanisms for robustness. VeraCrypt is optional - the service gracefully handles cases where it's not installed.
