member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyService
member.summary:
Service for YubiKey hardware token integration. Provides hardware-based authentication using YubiKey's FIDO2 capabilities.
Requirements: - YubiKey firmware 5.0 or later for FIDO2 support - Yubico.YubiKey SDK (already included as NuGet package) - User must touch the YubiKey to complete authentication
Note: This is a foundational implementation. Full FIDO2 credential management requires the Yubico.YubiKey.Fido2 namespace which is available but not yet fully integrated. For now, this service provides device detection and presence validation, with FIDO2 authentication marked for future enhancement.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyService.IsTokenPresent
member.summary: Determines whether a YubiKey hardware token is currently connected. Enumerates all connected YubiKey devices via USB/NFC.
member.returns: True if at least one YubiKey is detected; false otherwise.
member.remarks: This method is safe to call even if no YubiKey is present or the SDK encounters driver issues. It returns false rather than throwing exceptions to enable graceful degradation in UI workflows.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyService.Authenticate(System.Byte[],System.Byte[],System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Performs authentication with the YubiKey hardware token.
Current Implementation: This is a placeholder that verifies YubiKey presence. Full FIDO2 challenge-response authentication requires additional implementation using the Yubico.YubiKey.Fido2 namespace.
Future Enhancement: Will implement proper FIDO2 assertion verification following the pattern: 1. Load credential ID from manifest 2. Generate cryptographic challenge 3. Send GetAssertion command to YubiKey 4. Verify signature against stored public key
member.param: A cryptographic challenge (typically 32 bytes). If null or empty, a random challenge will be generated.
member.returns: A signed response from the YubiKey. Currently returns the SHA256 hash of the challenge as a placeholder. Will return actual FIDO2 assertion signature in future implementation.
member.exception: Thrown when no YubiKey is present or device communication fails.

remarks.para: Implementation Roadmap: - Phase 1 (Current): Device detection and presence validation - Phase 2 (Planned): FIDO2 credential registration during vault creation - Phase 3 (Planned): FIDO2 assertion verification during vault unlock - Phase 4 (Planned): PIN management and timeout handling

member.summary: Performs authentication with the YubiKey hardware token using FIDO2. This creates a cryptographic challenge and requires physical touch confirmation.
member.param: The FIDO2 credential ID from registration.
Optional PIN for YubiKey (null to prompt user or if PIN not set).
member.returns: FIDO2 assertion containing signature and authenticator data.
member.exception: If challenge or credentialId is null/empty.
If no YubiKey is present or FIDO2 is not supported.
If user doesn't touch YubiKey within timeout period.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyService.RegisterCredential(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Registers a new FIDO2 credential on the YubiKey for vault authentication. This method creates a resident credential that can be used for passwordless authentication.
member.param: Optional PIN for YubiKey (null if PIN not set).
member.returns: Credential result containing credential ID, public key, and serial number.
member.exception: If userId or userName is null/empty.
If no YubiKey is present or FIDO2 is not supported.
If user doesn't touch YubiKey within timeout period.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyService.VerifyAssertion(Yubico.YubiKey.Fido2.GetAssertionData,System.Byte[],System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Verifies a FIDO2 assertion signature against the stored public key.
member.param: The original challenge used for authentication.
member.returns: True if signature is valid; false otherwise.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyService.VerifyDeviceMatch(System.Int32)
member.summary: Verifies that a YubiKey matches the expected serial number.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyService.SetPin(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sets or changes the PIN on a YubiKey FIDO2 application.
member.param: The new PIN to set (must be 4-8 characters for most YubiKeys).
Current PIN (null if setting PIN for first time).
member.exception: If new PIN is invalid.
If no YubiKey is present or operation fails.

member.summary: Gets the number of remaining PIN retry attempts before the YubiKey locks.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyServiceImpl
member.summary: Production implementation of YubiKey integration using the official Yubico.YubiKey SDK. Supports FIDO2 authentication for vault unlocking. Requires YubiKey firmware 5.0 or later for full FIDO2 support. NOTE: This is a simplified implementation. Full FIDO2 integration requires additional work with the Yubico.YubiKey.Fido2 namespace.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyServiceImpl.IsTokenPresent
member.summary: Determines whether a YubiKey hardware token is currently connected. Enumerates all available YubiKey devices on the system.
member.returns: True if at least one YubiKey is present, false otherwise.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyServiceImpl.GetDeviceInfo
member.summary: Gets information about the first connected YubiKey device. Useful for displaying device details to the user.
member.returns: Tuple of (serialNumber, firmwareVersion, hasOtp, hasFido2) or null if no device.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyServiceImpl.Authenticate(System.String,System.Byte[],System.Byte[])
member.summary: Performs FIDO2 authentication using the YubiKey. This method generates a challenge, sends it to the YubiKey, and verifies the signed response. The user must touch the YubiKey to complete the authentication. NOTE: This is a stub. Full implementation requires Yubico.YubiKey.Fido2 APIs.
member.param: The credential ID from registration (stored in manifest).
A 32-byte random challenge for this authentication attempt.
member.returns: The signed authentication response, or null if authentication failed.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyServiceImpl.RegisterCredential(System.String,System.Byte[],System.String,System.Byte[])
member.summary: Registers a new FIDO2 credential on the YubiKey. This should be called during vault provisioning to bind the vault to this specific YubiKey. The returned credential ID must be stored in the vault manifest. NOTE: This is a stub. Full implementation requires Yubico.YubiKey.Fido2 APIs.
member.param: User identifier (can be the vault ID).
member.returns: The credential ID that must be stored for future authentication.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyServiceImpl.GenerateTotpCode(System.String)
member.summary: Generates a TOTP code if the YubiKey has OATH applet configured. This is an alternative to FIDO2 for two-factor authentication.
member.returns: A 6-digit TOTP code, or null if not available.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.YubiKeyServiceImpl.ResetFido2Application
member.summary: Resets the FIDO2 application on the YubiKey. WARNING: This will delete all stored credentials! Only use this for testing or if the user has lost access to their vault and needs to re-provision. NOTE: This is a stub. Full implementation requires Yubico.YubiKey.Fido2 APIs.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Services.ZeroKnowledge.IZkVaultService
member.summary: Zero-knowledge vault service interface providing secure file access with automatic key management and secure cleanup. All cryptographic operations are abstracted away from UI code.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Services.ZeroKnowledge.IZkVaultService.UnlockWithHybridKeyAsync(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Unlocks the vault using a pre-computed hybrid DEK (Data Encryption Key). This is used for Phase 2 post-quantum hybrid encryption where the DEK is derived from KEK ⊕ ML-KEM shared secret.
member.returns: True if unlock successful, false otherwise

Entry name within container, or null for single files
member.returns: Read-only memory stream containing decrypted file data

member.summary: Extracts a file from the vault to a secure temporary location. File is automatically deleted after TTL expires. Temp directory is restricted to current user and contents are securely wiped.

member.summary: Zero-knowledge vault service implementation. Manages master key lifecycle, provides secure file access, and ensures proper cleanup of sensitive data. All plaintext is wiped from memory after use.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Options.SecurityOptions
member.summary: Security configuration options for PhantomVault including tamper detection, decoy vault behavior, and defense mechanisms.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Options.SecurityOptions.AutoActivateDecoyOnTamper
member.summary: Enable automatic decoy vault activation when tampering is detected. Default: TRUE (enabled for maximum security). When enabled, the vault will automatically switch to showing fake credentials when debuggers, DLL injection, or memory manipulation is detected.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Options.SecurityOptions.DecoyCredentialCount
member.summary: Number of fake credentials to generate in the decoy vault. Valid range: 10-50. Default: 25. Higher numbers make the decoy more convincing but slightly slower to generate.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Options.SecurityOptions.LogDecoyActivation
member.summary: Whether to log decoy activation events to the audit log. Default: TRUE. WARNING: Sophisticated attackers who check logs may discover the decoy is active. Disable this for maximum stealth, or leave enabled for forensic evidence.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Options.SecurityOptions.AlertEmergencyContactOnDecoy
member.summary: Alert trusted emergency contact when decoy vault is activated. Default: FALSE (requires emergency contact to be configured first). When enabled, sends silent alert via configured channel (email, SMS, etc.).

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Options.SecurityOptions.DecoyAlertFilePath
member.summary: Path to store emergency decoy activation notifications. If set, a hidden file will be created when decoy activates for later forensic analysis. Example: "C:\Users\Username\.phantom_security_alert"

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Options.SecurityOptions.DecoyRandomSeed
member.summary: Seed for deterministic decoy credential generation. If null (default), credentials are randomly generated each time. Set a specific seed to generate the same fake credentials consistently.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Options.SecurityOptions.EnableTamperDetection
member.summary: Enable tamper detection monitoring. Default: TRUE. When enabled, continuously monitors for debuggers, DLL injection, memory manipulation.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Options.SecurityOptions.TamperDetectionIntervalSeconds
member.summary: Tamper detection check interval in seconds. Default: 10 seconds. Lower values provide faster detection but slightly higher CPU usage.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Options.SecurityOptions.Validate
member.summary: Validates the security options and throws if any are invalid.
member.exception: Thrown when values are outside valid ranges.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Options.VaultOptions
member.summary: Holds configuration parameters for vault operations. These options should be supplied via dependency injection or configured per user environment. Avoid hard‑coding sensitive paths here.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Options.VaultOptions.VeraCryptPath
member.summary: Gets or sets the absolute path to the VeraCrypt executable. This must be set before calling methods.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Options.VaultOptions.MountRoot
member.summary: Gets or sets the base directory used when mounting volumes on Unix‑like systems. For example "/mnt" or "/media/username". On Windows this property is ignored and drive letters are used instead.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.HybridKeyDerivation
member.summary:
Utilities for deriving hybrid encryption keys that combine classical password-based key derivation (Argon2id) with post-quantum key encapsulation mechanisms (ML-KEM-768).
The hybrid approach provides defense-in-depth: an attacker must break both the classical password scheme AND the quantum-resistant KEM to compromise the vault.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.HybridKeyDerivation.DeriveHybridKey(System.Byte[],System.Byte[])
member.summary:
Derives a hybrid encryption key by XORing a traditional key (from Argon2id) with a quantum-resistant shared secret (from ML-KEM). This provides dual resistance: classical password-based security plus post-quantum key exchange.
Security Properties: - If Argon2id is secure, the hybrid key is secure - If ML-KEM is secure, the hybrid key is secure - An attacker must break BOTH to compromise the key - XOR is information-theoretically secure combiner (one-time pad)
member.returns: 32-byte hybrid Data Encryption Key (DEK).
member.exception: If either input is not exactly 32 bytes.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.HybridKeyDerivation.SerializeEncryptionResult(PhantomVault.Core.Services.EncryptionResult)
member.summary: Serializes an EncryptionResult to Base64 string for storage in manifest. Format: nonce|tag|ciphertext (all Base64 encoded, pipe-separated)

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.HybridKeyDerivation.DeserializeEncryptionResult(System.String)
member.summary: Deserializes a Base64 string back to EncryptionResult.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.HybridKeyDerivation.ZeroMemory(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Securely zeros a byte array to remove sensitive key material from memory. This is a best-effort operation as the GC may have already copied the data.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.HybridKeyDerivation.ZeroMemory(System.Byte[][])
member.summary: Securely zeros multiple byte arrays in a single call. Useful for cleanup in finally blocks.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecureCredentialString
member.summary: Secure wrapper for credential strings that ensures sensitive data is encrypted in memory and properly zeroized when disposed.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecureCredentialString.GetPlaintext
member.summary: Retrieves plaintext value. CALLER MUST ZERO THE RETURNED ARRAY.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecureCredentialString.UseSecurely(System.Action{System.String})
member.summary: Executes action with plaintext and immediately zeros it.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecureMemory
member.summary: Provides utilities for handling sensitive data in memory. On supported platforms this includes locking pages to prevent swapping and clearing buffers after use. These APIs are best‑effort; if the underlying platform does not support mlock/VirtualLock the calls will be no‑ops.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecureMemory.Lock(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Locks the specified buffer into physical memory, preventing it from being paged to disk. Returns true on success or false if the platform does not support locking.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecureMemory.Unlock(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Unlocks the specified buffer, allowing it to be paged out.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecureMemory.SecureClear(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Securely zeros a byte array. Uses the built‑in CryptographicOperations.ZeroMemory which is immune to compiler optimisations that might skip clearing.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePassword
M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePassword.FromString(System.String)
member.summary:
Creates a SecurePassword from a string. The string parameter will still remain in memory (this is a .NET limitation), but the SecurePassword instance can be reliably zeroed.
For best security, use this immediately when receiving user input and do not store the original string.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePassword.FromSecureString(System.Security.SecureString)
member.summary: Creates a SecurePassword from a SecureString (for compatibility with legacy code that uses SecureString).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePassword.Empty
member.summary: Creates an empty SecurePassword (useful for keyfile-only authentication).

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePassword.AsSpan
member.summary: Returns a read-only span over the password characters. This span is only valid while the SecurePassword is not disposed.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePassword.Length
member.summary: Returns the length of the password.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePassword.IsEmpty
member.summary: Checks if the password is empty.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePasswordCombiner
member.summary: Securely combines a passphrase with keyfile contents for multi-factor authentication. Unlike string concatenation, this class ensures all intermediate buffers are properly zeroed after use.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePasswordCombiner.Combine(PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePassword,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Combines a passphrase with optional keyfile contents. The resulting buffer can be securely zeroed when disposed.
member.param: The user passphrase (can be empty for keyfile-only).
If true, throws if keyfile path is not provided.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePasswordCombiner.Combine(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Convenience method for backward compatibility with string passphrases. NOTE: The string passphrase will remain in memory - prefer using SecurePassword directly.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePasswordCombiner.AsSpan
member.summary: Returns a read-only span over the combined secret. This span is only valid while the combiner is not disposed.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.Utils.SecurePasswordCombiner.Length
member.summary: Returns the length of the combined secret.

member.name: T:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile
member.summary: Represents the USB cryptographic key file (usb_key.json) stored on authorized USB devices. SECURITY: Provides cryptographic authentication of USB devices beyond serial number validation.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.KeyId
member.summary: Unique identifier for this USB key. Must match one of the RequiredKeyIds in the policy.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.Label
member.summary: User-friendly label for this USB key (e.g., "Primary Key", "Backup Key #2").

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.PublicKey
member.summary: Ed25519 public key (base64-encoded) for verifying this USB key's authenticity. The private key should be held by the organization/administrator.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.Signature
member.summary: Digital signature of the key file, signed by the root certificate. This prevents unauthorized USB key creation.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.IssuedAt
member.summary: Timestamp when this key was issued (Unix seconds).

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.ExpiresAt
member.summary: Optional expiration timestamp (Unix seconds). 0 = no expiration.

member.name: P:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.VolumeSerial
member.summary: Volume serial number this key is bound to (optional). Provides additional binding to specific physical USB device.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.LoadAndVerify(System.String,System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa)
member.summary: Loads and verifies a USB key file from JSON.
member.returns: Validated UsbKeyFile instance
member.exception: If signature verification fails

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.VerifyKeyFileSignature(PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile,System.Security.Cryptography.ECDsa)
member.summary: Verifies the digital signature on the USB key file.

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.ValidateAgainstPolicy(ObscuraPolicy,System.String)
member.summary: Validates that this USB key matches the policy requirements.
member.param: Actual volume serial number of the USB device
member.returns: True if validation passes

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.ValidatePublicKey
member.summary: Validates the Ed25519 public key format.
member.returns: True if public key is valid base64-encoded 32-byte Ed25519 key

member.name: M:PhantomVault.Core.UsbKeyFile.ToJson(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Converts the USB key file back to JSON format.

member.name: T:PolicyEngine
member.summary: Enforces USB, manifest, and desktop sync policies for PhantomVault. Provides event-based notification for policy violations that require UI action.

member.name: E:PolicyEngine.LockUIRequested
member.summary: Event raised when the UI should be locked due to a policy violation. Subscribe to this in the UI layer to handle lockdown scenarios.

member.name: E:PolicyEngine.ZeroSecretsRequested
member.summary: Event raised when sensitive data should be cleared from memory. Pass byte arrays or char arrays to be zeroed via the event args.

member.name: T:PolicyEngine.SecretZeroEventArgs
member.summary: Registers sensitive data buffers to be zeroed when ZeroSecretsRequested is raised.

member.name: P:PolicyEngine.SecretZeroEventArgs.SecretsCleared
member.summary: Gets whether secrets have been successfully zeroed. Set this to true after clearing sensitive data in your handler.

member.name: M:PolicyEngine.IsUsbStandardSatisfied(System.IO.DriveInfo,System.String)
member.summary: Checks if the USB device meets the minimum USB standard requirement. Uses WMI to query USB controller properties and determine USB version.

member.name: M:PolicyEngine.GetUsbVersionFromPnpDevice(System.String)
member.summary: Determines USB version by examining the device's parent USB controller. Returns the detected USB version (2.0, 3.0, 3.1, 3.2) or 2.0 as default.

member.name: M:PolicyEngine.ValidateCryptoKeyUsb(System.IO.DriveInfo,System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Validates USB device in CryptoKey mode by loading and verifying usb_key.json. SECURITY: Requires cryptographic authentication of USB device.
member.param: Output parameter for the validated key ID
member.returns: True if USB key file is valid and matches policy

member.name: M:PolicyEngine.OnLockUIRequested
member.summary: Raises the LockUIRequested event to notify UI layer to lock the interface.

member.name: M:PolicyEngine.OnZeroSecretsRequested
member.summary: Raises the ZeroSecretsRequested event to notify handlers to clear sensitive memory. Also performs local memory zeroing for any buffers tracked by the policy engine.

member.name: T:PolicySynchronizer
member.summary: Coordinator for synchronized policy enforcement across USB, Desktop, and Manifest policies. Ensures consistent security posture and cross-policy validation.

member.name: M:PolicySynchronizer.SynchronizeAndValidate
member.summary: Performs comprehensive policy synchronization and validation. Ensures all policies work together coherently.
